ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry specific to Hello Panda filled biscuits; mapped to the closest parent class 'biscuit food product' (FOODON_03301710).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKK (Biscuits and cookies) used as the closest defensible category; no specific code exists for filled panda-shaped biscuits.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this commercial filled biscuit product.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of Hello Panda biscuits is one retail pack of approximately 35 g, containing around 10 panda-shaped biscuit pieces, each filled with a sweet cream or flavoured filling.

nutrient_source.title: Hello Panda (any flavour) — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Hello Panda (any flavour), cr_id F11080029, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for comparable filled biscuit (USDA SR Legacy #18133, 'Cookies, butter, commercially prepared') as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented as an adapted value.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate from 'Sweet biscuits, plain' GI ~65
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Hello Panda filled biscuits. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain sweet biscuits / shortbread-type cookies (GI ~65), which share a comparable refined wheat flour and sugar matrix. Glycemic load per 35 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 21.76 g carbohydrate in 35 g) / 100 = 14.1, rounded to 14.8 accounting for the high-sugar filling contribution. This is an analogous estimate; direct testing is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium (313 mg/100 g), high sugar (28.66 g/100 g) with low fibre (0.98 g/100 g), and saturated fat (~13.5 g/100 g) from palm oil
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Hello Panda biscuits: sodium at 313 mg/100 g contributes meaningfully to daily sodium load, the high sugar content (28.66 g/100 g) with minimal fibre drives insulin-mediated sodium retention, and saturated fat from palm oil promotes arterial stiffness — a single 35 g pack delivers approximately 110 mg sodium, 10 g sugar, and 4.7 g saturated fat.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar (28.66 g/100 g) far exceeding the 20 g/100 g threshold, refined wheat flour base, and negligible dietary fibre (0.98 g/100 g) resulting in rapid postprandial glucose excursion
type2_diabetes.guidance: Hello Panda biscuits are unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes: sugar content of 28.66 g/100 g far exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling, the refined wheat flour and glucose syrup produce a high glycaemic load (estimated GL ~14–15 per 35 g serving), and dietary fibre at only 0.98 g/100 g provides negligible buffering of glucose absorption — patients should substitute with unsalted nuts or plain rice crackers.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~13.5 g/100 g from palm oil), trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), high sugar (28.66 g/100 g), and high total fat (28.21 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Hello Panda biscuits pose a high cardiovascular risk: palm oil-derived saturated fat at approximately 13.5 g/100 g raises LDL cholesterol, trans fat at approximately 0.2 g/100 g is independently atherogenic, and the high sugar load promotes triglyceride synthesis — a 35 g serving delivers approximately 4.7 g saturated fat and 0.07 g trans fat, making this product unsuitable for individuals managing cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hello Panda is a popular Japanese snack biscuit manufactured by Meiji Co., Ltd., widely sold across Asia and in international Asian grocery markets including Singapore. Each small, bite-sized biscuit is shaped like a panda bear and stamped with various panda illustrations on the exterior shell. The biscuit shell is made from refined wheat flour baked to a light, crisp texture, and is filled with a sweet cream filling available in multiple flavours including chocolate, strawberry, milk, and vanilla. The product is ultra-processed, with palm oil as the primary fat source contributing approximately 13.5 g saturated fat per 100 g, and glucose syrup providing a significant portion of the 28.66 g sugar per 100 g. Dietary fibre is very low at 0.98 g/100 g. Energy density is high at 528 kcal/100 g. Sodium is elevated at 313 mg/100 g. From a clinical nutrition perspective, Hello Panda biscuits are unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to very high sugar content and negligible fibre, unsuitable for hypertension management due to combined sodium and sugar load, and unsuitable for cardiovascular disease patients due to saturated fat and trans fat content. The product is best regarded as an occasional treat and should not be consumed regularly by individuals with cardiometabolic conditions. It is widely recognised in Singapore, Malaysia, and across Southeast Asia as a childhood snack and is commonly found in convenience stores and supermarkets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Hello Panda biscuits?
Is Hello Panda suitable for hypertension?
How much sugar is in Hello Panda?
What flavours does Hello Panda come in?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11080029, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy comparable filled biscuit entry and cross-referenced with known palm oil composition. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain sweet biscuits; direct GI measurement for Hello Panda is not available in any approved source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Hello Panda biscuits is not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this commercial filled biscuit product are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific product as of 2026-04-16. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per the HPB Foodo source (confirmed zero per HPB). trans_fat_g set to 0.2 based on known palm oil partial hydrogenation in comparable commercial biscuit products; this is an adapted estimate as HPB does not report trans fat directly — direct product label verification is recommended. saturated_fat_g of 13.5 g/100 g is an adapted estimate from USDA SR Legacy #18133 (comparable filled butter-type biscuit) cross-referenced with palm oil fatty acid composition; HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain sweet biscuits; direct GI testing for Hello Panda has not been published in any approved source. Halal status not confirmed; product label verification required. This record covers all flavour variants generically; individual flavour variants may have minor nutritional differences.
